heroHeadline: Day 641: El Mayo Sentencing Pushed to May 18 (Second Postponement); El Guano at Large After Inner Circle Decimated
heroSubtitle: 641 days since El Mayo's capture — Week of Apr 20–24 saw peak US-Mexico enforcement pressure: visa bans on 75 Sinaloa associates (Apr 20), twin federal operations captured El Guano's top 10 lieutenants in Badiraguato and Tamazula including right-hand 'Abel N' — El Guano evaded for the 7th time (Apr 22–23), OFAC sanctioned 23-person fentanyl precursor network spanning India/Guatemala/Mexico (Apr 24). El Mayo EDNY sentencing now confirmed for May 18, 2026 — second postponement, prosecution and defense jointly requesting delay to coordinate cooperation terms. Apr 25: Nine homicides across Sinaloa (highest nationally); Apr 26: One killed in Navolato armed ambush. Running conflict toll: ~2,425+ since Sep 9, 2024.
footerNote: Data sourced from Noroeste, Riodoce, Proceso, InSight Crime, Reuters, AP, Animal Político, ACLED, DEA, SEDENA, SEMAR, Al Jazeera, AFP, Infobae, La Jornada, CBC News, Excelsior, El Diario del Noroeste, White House ONDCP, DOJ, El Informador, El Imparcial, Latin Times, Zeta Tijuana, NBC Los Angeles, KTLA, Washington Times, NBC Chicago, UPI, US State Department, CNN, Washington Post, CBS News, Expansión Política, La Razón, El Financiero, Latinus, Ejecentral, El Diario de Chihuahua, NTD, Eurasia Review, Ground News, El Sol de Sinaloa
